distribution.text: Copyright © Red Hat, Inc. All rights reserved.

notes.text:
An update for the ruby:3.3 module is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact of Moderate. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from the CVE link(s) in the References section.
notes.title: Topic

notes.text:
Ruby is an extensible, interpreted, object-oriented, scripting language. It has features to process text files and to perform system management tasks.

Security Fix(es):

* resolv: Denial of Service in resolv gem (CVE-2025-24294)

* rexml: REXML denial of service (CVE-2025-58767)

For more details about the security issue(s), including the impact, a CVSS score, acknowledgments, and other related information, refer to the CVE page(s) listed in the References section.
notes.title: Details

notes.text: A denial of service flaw was found in resolv ruby gem. This flaw allows an attacker to craft a malicious DNS packet containing a highly compressed domain name. When the resolv library parses this packet, the name decompression process consumes a large amount of CPU resources, as the library does not limit the resulting length of the name.
notes.title: Vulnerability description

notes.text: resolv: Denial of Service in resolv gem
notes.title: Vulnerability summary

notes.text: The CVSS score(s) listed for this vulnerability do not reflect the associated product's status, and are included for informational purposes to better understand the severity of this vulnerability.
notes.title: CVSS score applicability

remediations.details:
For details on how to apply this update, which includes the changes described in this advisory, refer to:

https://access.redhat.com/articles/11258

Mitigation for this issue is either not available or the currently available options do not meet the Red Hat Product Security criteria comprising ease of use and deployment, applicability to widespread installation base or stability.

vulnerabilities.title: resolv: Denial of Service in resolv gem

cwe.name: Improper Restriction of Recursive Entity References in DTDs ('XML Entity Expansion')

notes.text: A denial of service flaw has been discovered in the rubygem REXML. Certain input can cause excess cpu usage and given sufficiently large input this can affect program performance.
notes.title: Vulnerability description

notes.text: Red Hat Product Security team has rated this vulnerability as having the 'Moderate' severity, with the final CVSSv3.1 score of 5.3 as the final impact in availability is restricted to the application consuming the REXML package and not the whole system.
notes.title: Statement

notes.text: The CVSS score(s) listed for this vulnerability do not reflect the associated product's status, and are included for informational purposes to better understand the severity of this vulnerability.
notes.title: CVSS score applicability

remediations.details:
For details on how to apply this update, which includes the changes described in this advisory, refer to:

https://access.redhat.com/articles/11258
The primary mitigation for this flaw is to avoid parsing XML documents originating from untrusted, unauthenticated, or unverified sources.

cwe.name: Improper Removal of Sensitive Information Before Storage or Transfer

notes.text: A flaw was found in the URI module. A remote attacker could exploit this vulnerability by using the `+` operator to combine Uniform Resource Identifiers (URIs). This bypasses a previous fix and can lead to the leakage of sensitive information, such as user credentials (passwords), from the original URI, resulting in credential exposure.
notes.title: Vulnerability description

notes.text: uri: URI module: Credential exposure via URI + operator
notes.title: Vulnerability summary

notes.text: This vulnerability is rated Moderate as it allows for credential exposure when the URI module's `+` operator is used to combine URIs. This flaw bypasses a previous fix, potentially leading to sensitive information leakage from the original URI in affected applications across Red Hat Enterprise Linux, OpenShift Container Platform, and other products utilizing the vulnerable URI module.
notes.title: Statement

notes.text: The CVSS score(s) listed for this vulnerability do not reflect the associated product's status, and are included for informational purposes to better understand the severity of this vulnerability.
notes.title: CVSS score applicability

remediations.details:
For details on how to apply this update, which includes the changes described in this advisory, refer to:

https://access.redhat.com/articles/11258

Mitigation for this issue is either not available or the currently available options do not meet the Red Hat Product Security criteria comprising ease of use and deployment, applicability to widespread installation base, or stability.

vulnerabilities.title: uri: URI module: Credential exposure via URI + operator
